lines: wind cuts low across the gorse and the wolf goes still
a dire wolf slips between two standing stones and waits
you hear claws on stone before you see the grey shape
sheep bones scatter as the pack circles the old cairn
heather bends flat where something heavy just crouched
a low growl rolls down the hill before the charge
fangs flash white against the dark shape of the wolf
the hill goes quiet and that quiet is the warning
a shadow breaks from the gorse and the chase begins
you plant your feet as the wolf closes the last steps
cold wind carries the smell of wet fur and old blood
the wolf circles wide then cuts straight for your leg
stone teeth of the cairn cast long shadows at dusk
a howl answers from somewhere past the next ridge
you feel the ground shake as heavy paws hit the turf
the wolf snaps at empty air where your arm just was
tall grass hides three more shapes moving low and fast
a blade swings wide and the wolf twists just clear
you plant a boot on the cairn stone and swing hard
the pack spreads out to pin you against the ridge
grey fur bristles as the wolf reads your next move
wind howls through the gap where old bones still lie
you duck low as jaws snap shut just past your ear
the wolf lunges and you roll clear across wet grass
thunder of paws grows louder behind the standing stone
a snarl builds low in its throat before the leap
you swing hard and the wolf yelps and falls back
the hill goes dark as clouds roll over the ridge
claws rake stone as the wolf scrambles up the slope
you brace against a cairn as the pack closes in
a second wolf slides out from behind the gorse bush
teeth close on empty cloth as you twist free again
the wolf backs off then circles for another angle
cold rain slicks the grass where the wolf lost its grip
you hear bone crack under a heavy grey paw nearby
a low ridge hides the pack until the very last step
the wolf finally breaks and bolts across open moor
you land a hit right along its scarred grey flank
wind drops and the whole hill seems to hold its breath
the wolf snarls once then goes for your open side
a cairn stone shifts loose as you brace your weight
you spot the alpha wolf watching from the far ridge
jaws snap shut an inch from your trailing boot heel
the wolf drags low through heather to stay hidden
you swing again and this time the blow lands true
a whole pack of eyes glow faint in the fading light
the wolf skids on wet stone and loses its footing
you feel hot breath before you see the grey muzzle
the hill wind carries a long low howl toward you
claws dig deep into turf as the wolf pushes off hard
you step back onto solid stone and set your stance
the wolf circles the cairn twice before it commits
a final lunge and the wolf goes down in the grass
quiet returns to the hill once the last wolf yields
the harpy circles above the wind scoured hill
a cairn of stones marks where the harpy waits
gorse and heather hide the harpy from your eyes
wind claws at the ragged cliffs where it perches
sheep bones scatter across the old stone shelf
the harpy screams and drops from a heather ledge
tumbled stones crack beneath your boots tonight
its wings beat hard against the scoured hilltop
you climb the cairn while the harpy circles low
talons rake the air above the windy ridge
a shriek rolls down across the tumbled stones
the harpy dives and you duck behind a cairn
bones and heather crunch beneath your steps
wind howls across the hill where the harpy waits
you brace as feathers slice through the cold air
the cairn topples and stones roll down the slope
its claws grip the rock ledge above the gorse
you dodge as the harpy screams and swoops again
heather bends flat beneath the beating wings
the harpy perches high on the tallest stone
sheep bones crack under your boots as you run
wind drags heavy clouds across the green hill
you strike as the harpy folds its ragged wings
stones shift and tumble down the steep hillside
the harpy circles wide then drops like a stone
gorse scratches your arms as you climb higher
a cold wind carries the harpy shriek far away
you grip your blade as talons rake toward you
the cairn stands alone on the wind swept ridge
feathers drift down onto the tumbled stones
the harpy screeches and the hillside falls quiet
you duck low as claws sweep past your shoulder
heather and bone litter the old sheep trail
wind bites at your hands on the high stone ledge
the harpy folds its wings and drops from the sky
you climb past cairns toward the circling shriek
stones crack loose and tumble past your boots
its shadow crosses the hill before the dive
you swing hard as the harpy shrieks and falls
the wind carries bone dust across the ridge
gorse hides a nest tucked into the old cairn
talons flash and you roll behind a standing stone
the harpy rises again above the ragged cliff
heather whips sideways in the cold hill wind
you press on past bones bleached by the sun
the harpy dives twice before you land a hit
wind scours the hilltop where the cairns stand
its scream echoes off the tumbled grey stones
you brace against gorse as the harpy circles
the ridge shakes as stones tumble down below
claws rake stone and sparks fly into the wind
the harpy wheels once then drops toward you
sheep bones crunch beneath the cold hill wind
you stand your ground as the harpy dives again
the wind bites hard as the harpy climbs higher
wind scours the hill and scatters the loose gorse
an ogre lumbers between two tumbled standing stones
sheep bones rattle in the wind above the cairn
the cairn looks freshly disturbed and very haunted
a club swings wide and you duck under the arc
heather bends flat where something heavy walked
you feel the ground shake before the ogre turns
grey stone circles hide old bones beneath the moss
the ogre grips a club studded with rusted nails
a longsword connects and the ogre finally staggers
sloughs of loose scree slide down the steep slope
somebody buried something deep beneath that cairn
the ogre points a thick finger straight at you
torches flare as the party spreads across the hill
old cairns line the ridge like broken grey teeth
you dodge sideways as the club smashes the ground
gorse snags your boot as you scramble up the hill
the ogre lurches forward with a low ugly grunt
a dagger flashes and finds the gap in its armor
wind carries the smell of old bones off the ridge
the hollowed cairn sits open and freshly disturbed
you circle wide while the ogre swings its club
loot spills from a sack tied to the ogre belt
a rogue slips behind the ogre without a sound
the ogre roars and the whole hillside seems to shake
your blade catches the light before it strikes true
heather and gorse hide a dozen scattered bones
the ogre drags a club that leaves furrows in dirt
you plant your boot and swing with everything you have
a shield rings out as the club crashes against it
the wind howls across the hill and the ogre roars
grey light spills over the ridge as the fight begins
you dart backward as loose stones tumble past
the cairn hums with something older than the ogre
a bard hums low while the ogre lumbers closer
the ogre grabs a stone and hurls it at your head
sheep skulls sit stacked beside the burial cairn
you feel the club whistle just past your ear
the ogre stumbles on the loose scattered scree
a wizard flings light across the darkening hill
the ogre swings twice and misses twice in a row
stones shift underfoot as the ogre charges forward
you brace against the wind and raise your blade
the ogre snarls and swings its club overhead
loot glints beneath a pile of tumbled stones
the hill smells of wet heather and old bones
a hidden dagger finds the ogre soft underarm
the ogre staggers back and drops to one knee
wind bends the grass flat around the old cairn
you roll aside as the club splits the ground
the ogre grips its club and turns to face you
grey clouds roll low over the wind scoured hill
a final blow drops the ogre onto the stones
a knee buckles and the ogre finally goes down
wind scours the hill where the old stones lean
gorse and heather hide the cairn from view
a green hag hums low among the sheep bones
her fingers curl and the fog thickens fast
sheep bones rattle across the tumbled stones
she laughs and the mist rolls down the slope
your blade finds the gap before she turns
cairn stones shift as the hag steps closer
heather bends low beneath a cold green light
she offers a trade you should never take
torches flare against the creeping green fog
a hook of a hand reaches through the mist
old bones crunch beneath the wind swept grass
the hag grins wide and her knuckles crack
green light pools where the standing stones lean
you dodge as her stew pot begins to steam
sheep skulls line the path up the windy hill
she hisses kindly words that hide a trick
gorse thorns catch you as the fog rolls in
a rattled breath escapes the hollow cairn
her smile splits wide beneath the hood
the hill hums with something old and hungry
green mist eats the light around the stones
a dagger of wind cuts across the ridge
she sings low and the sheep bones shiver
your feet slip on wet stones near the cairn
the hag steps sideways through the swirling fog
bones and heather crunch beneath your boots
she offers a fistful of tricks and lies
cold wind hisses through the tumbled stones
a shot of green light flares near the cairn
her laugh rattles across the empty hill
sheep bones clatter as the mist rolls thick
the standing stones lean toward the fight
green eyes glance up from beneath the hood
she stirs a pot that reeks of old magic
your blade rakes across her twisted grip
the hag mutters and the fog thickens more
wind and rain scour the old grey cairn
a green glow pulses beneath the hillside
she steps through illusions of stone and grass
your torch gutters in the rising green mist
the sheep bones hum a low and eerie tune
a hag of green skin waits among the stones
cairn shadows twist as the wind picks up
she trades kind words for something cruel
the hill grows quiet before the hag attacks
your grip tightens as the mist grows thick
green fingers curl around a rusted dagger
sheep bones glow faintly in the fading light
she cackles as the fog swallows the hill
wind bites hard across the lonely ridge
the hag melts into a swirl of green smoke
old stones hum as the battle finally turns
a green shimmer hangs low over the cairn
she waves a hand and the grass bends flat
Two heads of the ettin argue over which arm swings first.
Wind scours the hilltop as the ettin drags its club free.
A cairn stone cracks under the ettin heavy boot.
Gorse snaps flat where the ettin second head turns to look.
You duck as a club the size of a door sweeps past.
Sheep bones rattle when the ettin stomps toward the stones.
One head laughs while the other head roars at you.
Heather bends low under the shadow of two grinning heads.
A club drags a long furrow through the wet hill grass.
The standing stones shiver each time the ettin bellows.
Its left hand hefts a boulder still gray with lichen.
You feel the ground shake with every ettin footstep.
Two voices argue over which of you to crush first.
A gust carries the reek of old bones and mud.
The ettin right head grins while the left head snarls.
Cairn dust drifts as the ettin lumbers past a sheep skull.
You sidestep a swing that flattens a patch of gorse.
Its knuckles drag deep grooves across the hillside.
A second grin spreads across the ettin other face.
You brace as both clubs rise above the tumbled stones.
The hill wind howls between the ettin gnashing teeth.
Bone fragments crunch beneath its enormous heel.
Its shadow swallows the cairn and half the slope.
You watch two heads argue about which target to smash.
A boulder thuds down where you stood a breath ago.
The ettin drags a club and grins with both mouths.
Torn heather scatters as the ettin turns to face you.
You hear both heads snarl the same warning at once.
Its club splinters an ancient standing stone in half.
A gust of cold wind rides ahead of the ettin charge.
You duck low as two fists swing from either side.
The hill trembles as the ettin plants its heavy stance.
Sheep bones crunch and scatter under its dragging club.
A rough voice argues with itself over the next blow.
You brace your grip as the ettin roars from both throats.
Its second head spits a curse at the gray sky above.
Rain slicks the gorse as the ettin lifts its club high.
You watch a boulder arc over the tumbled cairn stones.
The ettin drags both feet through mud and old bone.
One head grins wide while a club swings toward you.
Wind bends the heather flat before the ettin charge.
You feel the club whistle past your ear and hair.
Its huge shoulders block the pale hill sky entirely.
The cairn crumbles as the ettin leans against the stone.
You watch two mouths snarl a single word together.
A club grazes stone and sends sparks into the wind.
The hill fog thickens around the ettin lumbering shape.
Its second head barks orders the first head ignores.
You brace as the ettin drags its club through the mud.
Bones scatter beneath the ettin heavy dragging stride.
The wind carries a low growl from both its throats.
A cairn stone topples as the ettin shoulder rams past.
You watch the ettin plant its boots among old bones.
The hill goes quiet before the ettin final furious swing.
